_doc: PD verification policy — the single source of truth for every numeric constant used by pd_verification.py. Nothing numeric is hardcoded in the Python. Override precedence (lowest to highest): this shipped file → a copy placed at /work/verification_policy.json (the ./work volume mount, editable on the host without rebuilding the container; path overridable via PDV_POLICY_FILE) → environment variables named PDV_<KEY> (e.g. PDV_MAX_CONGESTION_ITERATIONS=80). Keys starting with '_' are documentation and are ignored by the loader.
_doc_die_density_limit_pct: Step 1 of the routing-DRC diagnosis: if synthesis chip area / die area exceeds this percentage, the die is the root cause and everything else is skipped.
_doc_die_density_target: When resizing the die, target this cell-density fraction: target_side = ceil(sqrt(chip_area / die_density_target) * die_margin_factor).
_doc_congestion: Shorts-dominant escalation: bump congestion_iterations by congestion_iteration_step, capped at max_congestion_iterations, before ever touching placement.
_doc_density: Next escalation: reduce placement density by density_step, never below density_floor.
_doc_violation_dominance_fraction: A violation category (Short / Metal Spacing) 'dominates' when it exceeds this fraction of all typed violations.
_doc_util_warning_fraction: Utilization check turns 'warning' above util_warning_fraction * the user's max_util_pct threshold (still 'ok' below it, 'error' above the threshold itself).
_doc_default_clock_period_ns: Fallback clock period used in fix arithmetic when run_meta.json predates clock_period_ns being recorded.
_doc_overflow_fraction_detect_max: Log-parsing unit detection: gpl reports overflow as a 0-1 fraction; values at or below this bound are converted to percent.
_doc_rounding: Decimal places for proposed config values (these end up in the user's config, so their precision is policy too).
